content.text: Set your websites default content width. This sets the website's 'container' width and optimizes your fluid responsive typography.

content.title: Content Width
content.tooltip: Set the max-width of the website's content.

content.title: Minimum Width
content.tooltip: Set the max-width of the website's content.

content.text: Sticky and overlay headers can cause layout collisions. ACSS can help prevent this if you tell ACSS your header height. Check the docs for more information.

content.title: Header Height (Desktop)
content.tooltip: The current height of your header on desktop devices.

content.title: Header Height (Mobile)
content.tooltip: The current height of your header on mobile devices.

content.text: Do you want ACSS to manage your content and scroll margin offsets automatically?

content.title: Offset Content Automatically
content.tooltip: Fixes spacing issues with sticky and overlay headers. Will not work if a header height isn't set.

content.title: Offset Scroll Margin Automatically
content.tooltip: Use header height for scroll margins (scroll to hash).

content.text: You can remove the offset by targeting specific pages or headers.

content.title: Exclude Headers
content.tooltip: When these header selectors are present, we'll deactivate content offset.

content.title: Exclude Pages
content.tooltip: Turn off the offset on the following pages.

content.text: Content Grid is an advanced layout system particularly useful for 'breakout' content and blog post layouts. Check the docs and videos to learn more.

content.title: Content Grid
content.tooltip: Enable Content Grid utilities.

content.title: Default Sections to Content Grid
content.tooltip: Replace default section structure with content grid.

content.title: Feature Width
content.tooltip: Sets the width of the 'feature' zone.

content.title: Feature Max Width
content.tooltip: Sets the width of the 'feature-max' zone (value is added to the 'feature' zone value).

content.title: Exclude Sections
content.tooltip: Exclude sections from defaulting to content grid.

content.text: Auto Grids are minimalistic, automatically responsive grids that don't require breakpoints. Use this panel to adjust some of the default settings.

content.title: Flow Option
content.tooltip: How should grid items flow in auto grids by default? 'Fit' instructs items to grow to take available space (first row only) and 'fill' will behave like a traditional grid.

content.title: Aggressiveness
content.tooltip: How soon should items stack (0 to 1 in decimal). Higher values stack items sooner.

content.title: Ratio Breakpoint
content.tooltip: At what screen width (in pixels) should auto-ratio grids (2-3, 3-2, etc.) stack?

content.text: Boxed Layouts set a max-width along with additional visual styling on the 'body' element of your website. It's used to create a specific look and feel and helps ensure design consistency on larger screens.

content.title: Boxed Layout
content.tooltip: Enable boxed layout style for the website.

content.title: Device Background
content.tooltip: Sets HTML background color.

content.title: Top Margin
content.tooltip: Adds spacing above your boxed layout.

content.title: Border Style
content.tooltip: Supports multi-axis values (top right bottom left).

content.title: Border Width
content.tooltip: Supports multi-axis values (top right bottom left).

content.title: Border Color
content.tooltip: Supports multi-axis values (top right bottom left).

content.title: Border Radius
content.tooltip: Supports multi-axis values (top right bottom left).
